eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Vivo Keyd Stars, Fluxo W7M, and RED Canids nearly even at around 47% implied probability to win CBLOL 2026 Split 1, underscoring the razor-thin margins in the looming double-elimination playoffs despite RED Canids' flawless 6-0 regular season record capped by a dominant 2-0 sweep of LOS last weekend. Fluxo W7M rides a three-series win streak after dismantling paiN Gaming 2-0, while Keyd Stars solidified its top-four contention with a clean 2-0 over Leviatán, highlighting consistent macro play and carry threats across the trio. The BO5 playoff format—with top-two byes to round two—amplifies upset potential, as recent head-to-heads and game differentials reveal parity in draft execution and clutch performances among these frontrunners.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 140-159 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: T20 Series Bangladesh vs Sri Lanka, Women: Bangladesh vs Sri Lanka
markets.description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Bangladesh and Sri Lanka scheduled for April 30 2026 in T20 Series Bangladesh vs Sri Lanka, Women.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sri Lanka Women hold a 1-0 lead in the three-match T20I series after a dominant 56-run victory in the 1st T20I at Sylhet International Cricket Stadium, where Harshitha Samarawickrama's 61 and Imesha Dulani's 55 powered them to 161/4, while Bangladesh Women collapsed to 105/6 chasing, losing early wickets despite Shorna Akter's unbeaten 36. Building momentum from their 2-1 ODI series win—capped by a 7-wicket triumph in the 3rd ODI—Sri Lanka's superior head-to-head record (11-2 in 13 T20Is) and Chamari Athapaththu's all-round contributions (32 & 2/14) underline trader consensus favoring them. Bangladesh's top-order fragility persists as a key vulnerability ahead of the 2nd and 3rd T20Is on April 30 and May 2 in Sylhet, with pitch conditions likely favoring pace in night games.

markets.question: Israel x Hamas ceasefire cancelled by June 30?
markets.description:
On October 9, 2025 Israel and Hamas signed a deal implementing a ceasefire: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/israel-hamas-agree-gaza-ceasefire-return-hostages-2025-10-09/

This market will resolve to "Yes" if a either Israel or Hamas announce the cancellation of the ongoing ceasefire, or if a consensus of credible reporting confirms that the ceasefire is no longer in effect, by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

Announcements that one side or another violated the ongoing ceasefire will not be enough to resolve this market to "Yes"; only a definitive announcement by Israel or Hamas, or otherwise a wide consensus of credible reporting that that the ceasefire has been ended, will qualify to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary solution resolution source will be official information from Hamas or the government of Israel, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The IDF reported 14 Hamas breaches from April 8-16 and five more April 17-21, amid ongoing Israeli airstrikes killing at least 32 Palestinians since early April; Hamas rejected full disarmament but signaled willingness to relinquish some weapons. U.S.-Hamas direct talks on April 15 yielded no breakthroughs, while regional ceasefires with Lebanon and Iran highlight Gaza's deadlock. Upcoming deadlines for compliance or Phase II implementation could trigger cancellation by either side or externally, underscoring the high risk of escalation.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) hit (LOW) $260 Week of April 27 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of April 27 2026, any 1-minute candle for Apple (AAPL) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the South Carolina Senate race in 2026?
markets.description:

A candidate shall be considered to represent a party in the event that he or she is the nominee of the party in question. Candidates other than the Democratic or Republican nominee (e.g., Greens, Libertarian, independent) may be added at a later date.

Candidates who run as independents will not be encompassed by the “Democrat” or “Republican” options regardless of any affiliation they may have with the party.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on the official certification.

Graham's fundraising edge—$20.7 million raised versus challenger Mark Lynch's $5.7 million—combined with Donald Trump's endorsement, bolsters his path despite recent polls showing a 41-21 primary lead. Paul Dans's April 10 withdrawal and endorsement of Lynch failed to consolidate opposition significantly. General election surveys, including a Democrat-sponsored Impact Research poll from late February to early March showing Graham ahead of Annie Andrews 47-42, underscore the state's partisan tilt favoring the incumbent despite narrow margins.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kharg Island, Iran's primary oil export terminal handling nearly 90% of its crude shipments, has faced repeated U.S. airstrikes on military targets—including bunkers, radar stations, and ammunition storage—most recently on April 7, when over 50 sites were hit without damaging energy infrastructure. Satellite imagery as of late April confirms ongoing oil loading operations despite a U.S. naval blockade and storage nearing capacity, pressuring Tehran's economy. President Trump's threats of seizure or full obliteration persist amid stalled diplomacy over the Strait of Hormuz, with Iran warning of retaliation against regional oil facilities. Traders monitor for escalation signals like ground operations or Houthi disruptions ahead of potential deadlines.

events.description:

If the Nuggets win, the market will resolve to "Nuggets".
If the Timberwolves win, the market will resolve to "Timberwolves".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: In their Western Conference first-round playoff series, the sixth-seeded Timberwolves hold a 3-2 lead over the third-seeded Nuggets after Denver's commanding 125-113 Game 5 victory on April 27, powered by Nikola Jokic's 27-point, 12-rebound, 16-assist triple-double. Minnesota's outlook darkened with Anthony Edwards sidelined week-to-week by a left knee hyperextension and bone bruise from Game 4, alongside Donte DiVincenzo's season-ending Achilles tear, forcing reliance on Ayo Dosunmu's heroics and Rudy Gobert's rim protection. Aaron Gordon's calf injury leaves him out for Game 6 at Target Center on April 30, testing Jamal Murray's scoring against Minnesota's home-court edge and defensive versatility in a potential closeout or series-forcing battle.

markets.question: Google (GOOGL) Up or Down on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Close price for Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) on April 30, 2026 is higher than the Close price for Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) on the most recent prior trading day.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the Close price for Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) on April 30, 2026 is lower than the Close price for Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) on the most recent prior trading day.

E.g., ordinarily, a market on Monday would refer to the previous Friday for its most recent closing price, unless that Friday were a market holiday, in which case it would refer to Thursday, or the next most recent trading day.

If the two specified closing prices are exactly equal, this market will resolve 50-50. Closing prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) does not trade at all during the regular session, the market will resolve 50-50.

For a standard full trading session, the closing price refers to the Pyth "Close" value of the 1-minute candle corresponding to the final minute of regular trading hours on the primary exchange.

If either of the relevant days has no valid Pyth Close value for the 1-minute candle corresponding to the end of regular trading hours on the primary exchange, the market will use the last valid Pyth price achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange as the effective closing price. If no valid Pyth price exists for that trading day due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption, the official closing price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine the closing price for that day.

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed security during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market will be Pyth, specifically the "Close" values for the relevant 1-minute candle available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.GOOGL%2FUSD. Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.GOOGL%2FUSD?t=1773432000).

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Shenzhen be 20°C or below on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shenzhen/ZGSZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from AccuWeather and Weather Channel consensus indicate a high temperature of 26-28°C (78-82°F) for Shenzhen on May 1, driven by lingering cloud cover and residual moisture from heavy rains and thundery showers that battered the region over the past week, including torrential downpours easing only on April 30. This follows a weak cold air mass that suppressed April highs to 22-26°C mid-month, below the early May climatological average of 28-29°C amid the subtropical transition to summer. Trader sentiment reflects this dampened warming potential, though clearing skies could boost peaks; watch China Meteorological Administration updates and new ECMWF/GFS runs for shifts before resolution via official Shenzhen Bao'an Airport observations.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democrat Analilia Mejia's commanding 94.5% implied probability for a 20-25% margin of victory in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election stems from post-April 16 vote tallies showing her lead at 20.7% as of April 24, bolstered by late-counted provisional and mail ballots that flipped key areas like Passaic County's portion—unprecedented for modern Democrats there. The safely Democratic district, where Kamala Harris led by roughly 12 points in 2024, saw Mejia overperform amid low-turnout conditions typical of House specials favoring incumbents. Weaker Republican turnout against challenger Joe Hathaway further entrenched her position. Realistic challenges include substantial remaining GOP-leaning ballots or certification disputes, though trends point to stability above 20%.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 Roland Garros Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 Roland Garros Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after June 21, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Roland Garros (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner's 65% implied probability as Roland Garros favorite reflects his breakout clay-court mastery, highlighted by his Monte-Carlo Masters title two weeks ago—dismantling Alexander Zverev 6-1, 6-4 in the semifinals before edging Carlos Alcaraz 7-6(5), 6-3 in the final to reclaim world No. 1 status—and his fresh semifinals run at the ongoing Mutua Madrid Open, joining Djokovic and Nadal in reaching last four at all nine ATP Masters 1000s. Alcaraz's wrist injury forcing withdrawals from Barcelona and Madrid, plus Djokovic's Madrid absence amid recovery concerns, clears Sinner's draw on the slower Paris surface. Zverev's consistent deep clay runs earn 10%, while Djokovic's 14 French Open finals experience holds 8.8% despite rust.

Teammate Michael Olise surges to 17.5% with 22 goals and 26 assists in 54 games, featuring crucial Champions League quarterfinal impacts like assisting Kane and scoring himself. Kylian Mbappé sits at 12.5% as Real Madrid trail Barcelona by nine points in La Liga standings, while Ousmane Dembélé (11.3%) and Lamine Yamal (8.5%, hampered by recent injury) chase amid competitive table battles. Champions League progression and the 2026 World Cup will shape final outcomes.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices "Nothing" at 85% for former President Obama facing no federal charges, arrest, or divorce through December 31, 2026, driven by the complete absence of official DOJ actions, indictments, or credible divorce filings in recent months. Over the past week, Obama's X post responding to the April 25 White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting—which prompted partisan criticism but no legal repercussions—and his April 18 Bronx childcare center visit with NYC Mayor Mamdani represent routine public engagements, far from triggering events. Unsubstantiated rumors of back-channel diplomacy or arrests lack primary confirmation, while Obama Foundation milestones like the June 19 Presidential Center opening sustain his low-drama profile amid 2026 midterm positioning. Absent late-breaking legal developments, structural barriers to "Something" remain high.

markets.question: Metamask FDV above $500M one day after launch?
markets.description:

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Metamask doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit (LOW) $300 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Tesla shares closed at $373.23 on April 29, 2026, embodying trader sentiment post-Q1 earnings on April 22, where non-GAAP EPS of $0.41 topped consensus estimates of $0.36 on $22.39 billion revenue, despite automotive deliveries missing expectations and net income rising 17% to $477 million. Key drivers include Cybercab robotaxi production ramp at Giga Texas this month and unsupervised Full Self-Driving deployment eyed for late 2026, counterbalancing capex surge above $25 billion annually. Analyst consensus price target hovers near $400 amid 15% revenue growth forecast to $108.9 billion for full-year 2026, with Q2 deliveries in July as the next catalyst influencing May price action.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the first-round Stanley Cup Playoffs series tied 2-2, Vegas Golden Knights command 59.5% implied probability as traders weigh their home-ice advantage for tonight's Game 5 at T-Mobile Arena and potential Game 7. Vegas' resilient 5-4 overtime win in Game 4—capped by Shea Theodore's game-winner after blowing a 3-0 lead—showcased depth from Jack Eichel, Brett Howden (2G-1A), and Carter Hart's 27/31 saves, shifting momentum despite William Karlsson's ongoing lower-body absence. Utah Mammoth impressed with their first home playoff victory in Game 3 behind Karel Vejmelka's 30 saves and Lawson Crouse's two goals, but Barrett Hayton's upper-body injury hampers their center depth on the road against Vegas' proven playoff pedigree from the 2023 Cup run.

markets.question: Will Republicanos (REPUBLICANOS) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

events.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Brazilian Senate, not by any federation or coalition it may be a part of.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Partido Liberal (PL) to secure the most seats in Brazil's October 4 Senate election renewing 54 positions across 27 states and the Federal District, with implied probabilities reflecting aggregate state-level polls projecting PL victories in multiple competitive races. Recent surveys, including Quaest in Bahia and Vox Brasil in São Paulo from late April, show PL candidates viable alongside frontrunners, bolstered by Flávio Bolsonaro's PL presidential campaign tying President Lula in Reuters and Datafolha polls as of April 27. NOVO and PSDB trail due to narrower regional strengths, such as in Minas Gerais, while PT lags amid weaker Senate showings despite Northeast dominance. Upcoming primaries and candidate announcements could shift dynamics in battleground states.

markets.question: Will Chicago Sky win the 2026 WNBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is determined as the champion of the WNBA for the 2026 season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed participant to named as the champion of WNBA for the 2026 season per the rules of WNBA (e.g., participant is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after November 14, 11:59 PM ET, or a champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the WNBA: however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Liberty lead trader consensus at 24.5% implied probability for the 2026 WNBA championship, bolstered by re-signing their Big Three—Breanna Stewart, Sabrina Ionescu, and Jonquel Jones—while adding Satou Sabally in free agency, maintaining roster continuity after their 2024 title. Indiana Fever trail closely at 22.5% following Aliyah Boston's record $6.3 million extension to pair with Caitlin Clark's Year 3 growth, amplified by their dominant 109-91 preseason victory over the Liberty on April 25 that highlighted offensive firepower. Defending champions Las Vegas Aces sit third at 15% amid potential depth concerns post-dynasty, with Atlanta Dream and Golden State Valkyries gaining traction from strong offseasons and expansion momentum, fostering league-wide parity as the May 8 opener nears.

markets.question: Will Adam Miller win the 2026 Los Angeles mayoral election?
markets.description:
The 2026 Los Angeles mayoral election will be held on June 2, 2026, to elect the mayor of Los Angeles, California. If no candidate receives a majority of the vote, a runoff election will be held on November 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate that wins the election.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting; however, if there is any ambiguity in the results, this market will resolve according to official information from the City of Los Angeles.

events.title: Los Angeles Mayoral Election
events.description:

This market will resolve according to the candidate that wins the election.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting; however, if there is any ambiguity in the results, this market will resolve according to official information from the City of Los Angeles.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices City Councilmember Nithya Raman as the frontrunner at 50.5% implied probability to win the 2026 Los Angeles mayoral election, driven by her recent fundraising surge alongside Spencer Pratt and momentum from a late-March poll showing her leading incumbent Mayor Karen Bass, despite high voter uncertainty with 40% undecided per the early-April UCLA Luskin survey. Bass trails at 23% amid unfavorable views exceeding 50% in some polling, reflecting criticism over homelessness and city management, while reality TV personality Pratt holds 20% as an "angry taxpayer" outsider appealing on crime but facing residency eligibility questions after relocating post-Palisades Fire. The nonpartisan June 2 primary will advance the top two to November, amplifying volatility from undecideds and upcoming debates.

markets.question: Will Al Shabab Saudi Club vs. Al Ittihad Saudi Club end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 10, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.
markets.groupItemTitle: Draw (Al Shabab Saudi Club vs. Al Ittihad Saudi Club)

events.title: Al Shabab Saudi Club vs. Al Ittihad Saudi Club
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Saudi Professional League game, scheduled for Friday, April 10, 2026 between Al Shabab Saudi Club and Al Ittihad Saudi Club.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Al Ittihad at 64% implied probability to win away at Al Shabab, driven by their solid sixth-place standing in the Saudi Pro League after 28 matches, contrasted with Al Shabab's mid-table struggles around 11th-12th. Ittihad's unbeaten run in the last five head-to-head meetings, including a 3-1 home victory earlier this season, bolsters sentiment, alongside their stronger recent form with four wins in six league games. Al Shabab's recent draws against Al Fateh, Al Qadsiah, and Al Riyadh underscore scoring and defensive frailties, exacerbated by long-term injuries to right-back Mohammed Harbush (cruciate ligament tear) and winger Carlos Junior (abdominal muscles). The 20% draw pricing reflects low expectations for a stalemate in this rivalry matchup.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Democratic Party at 92.5% implied probability in the IL-09 House race due to the district's strong partisan lean—Cook PVI D+19, where Kamala Harris carried 68% in 2024—and Daniel Biss's victory in the crowded March 17 Democratic primary, securing the nomination in the open seat left by retiring Rep. Jan Schakowsky. Biss holds a fundraising edge with over $267,000 cash on hand entering April, dwarfing Republican nominee John Elleson's resources, amid ratings of Solid Democratic from Cook Political Report and others. No general election polling exists yet, but the low-turnout GOP primary underscores weak opposition. Realistic challenges include a major Biss scandal, depressed Democratic turnout, or a powerful national Republican midterm wave before November 3.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Sao Paulo be 29°C on April 29?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Sao Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 29 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Sao Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/br/guarulhos/SBGR.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly favors 29°C (99.9%) as São Paulo's highest temperature on April 29, backed by official forecasts from the city's Centro de Gerenciamento de Emergências Climáticas (CGE) and Instituto Nacional de Meteorologia (INMET), which project a daytime maximum of 28–29°C. This positioning stems from a recent cold front's retreat into the Atlantic Ocean over the past 24 hours, ushering in a high-pressure ridge with clearing skies after morning cloudiness and isolated showers, light southeast winds, and humidity dropping to 55%. Observational data shows early morning lows near 19–20°C, with solar heating expected to peak this afternoon under partly cloudy conditions. Realistic challenges include prolonged cloud cover or unexpected rain suppressing the max below 28°C, or greater-than-forecast sunshine and urban heat effects pushing it to 30°C; INMET station readings through evening will finalize resolution amid typical short-range forecast uncertainty.

Jasmine Clark at 75.5% implied probability due to her dominant fundraising lead per recent FEC reports and over $1 million in super PAC support from Protect Progress. Educator Everton Blair Jr. holds second at 18.5% amid a crowded field lacking clear county-level endorsements, while recent Atlanta Press Club debates allowed candidates like Heavenly Kimes and Emanuel Jones to gain visibility but failed to erode Clark's financial edge. Incumbency vacuum and cash advantage historically favor frontrunners in Georgia primaries, though turnout in this Atlanta-area battleground could shift dynamics.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) close above $220 end of April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Apple (AAPL) on the final trading day of April 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/AAPL/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

eventMetadata.context_description: Apple (AAPL) shares closed at $269.77 on April 29, 2026, amid heightened volatility ahead of the fiscal Q2 earnings release after market close on April 30—the resolution date for end-of-April pricing. Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects positioning around expectations for iPhone revenue of approximately $56.5 billion and accelerating services growth, emphasized by the new CEO as the next engine, with recent UBS price target hike to reflect supply chain improvements for iPhone storage components. Analyst average targets stand at $296–$302, versus the current share price, supported by S&P 500 levels near 7,100; however, unusual pre-earnings put activity signals caution on potential misses amid broader market dynamics. Post-earnings guidance will influence May momentum, but April 30 close hinges on intraday sentiment.

markets.question: Will Benfica finish 2nd in the Primeira Liga for the 2025-26 season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club finishes 2nd in the final standings of the 2025-2026 Primeira Liga season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve to the team officially recognized by Primeira Liga as finishing in second place. If multiple teams are officially awarded second place, the market will resolve to the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to finish 2nd in the 2025-2026 Primeira Liga season (e.g. they are mathematically unable to achieve enough points), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025-2026 Primeira Liga season is cancelled, postponed after June 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no 2nd place team declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Primeira Liga. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Benfica's trader consensus lead at 69% for Primeira Liga second place stems from their dramatic 2-1 Lisbon Derby victory over Sporting CP on April 19, 2026—a 93rd-minute Rafa Silva winner that propelled the unbeaten Águias into second on the table after 31 matches, overtaking rivals with one game in hand. FC Porto's commanding first-place position, bolstered by recent draws like 0-0 against Sporting in the Taça de Portugal, renders their 0.9% slim hopes negligible barring collapses. Sporting's 30% reflects competitive form but vulnerability exposed in the derby loss, with three matches remaining critical for Champions League qualification amid Mourinho's invincible Benfica campaign.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 120-139 tweets from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 1 12:00 PM ET to May 8, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will "The Great Divide" - Noah Kahan debut week album sales be less than 125k?
markets.description:
Noah Kahan's new album 'The Great Divide' is scheduled to be released on April 24, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the debut week sales for Noah Kahan's album 'The Great Divide', according to Hits Daily Double.

If the album 'The Great Divide' has not been released by May 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.
 
If the album’s debut week sales fall exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the "HITS TOP 50" list found at https://hitsdailydouble.com/sales_plus_streaming, specifically, the figure in the column titled "Activity", once the results are finalized for the album’s debut week. If this resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, another credible resolution source may be chosen.

markets.question: Will Douglas Davis win the Newark mayoral election?
markets.description:
The 2025 Newark mayoral election will be held on May 12, 2026, with a potential runoff scheduled for June 9, 2026.

markets.question: Will Travis Kelce retire before next season?

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Austin be between 88-89°F on April 29?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Austin-Bergstrom International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 29 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Austin-Bergstrom International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/tx/austin/KAUS.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will monthly inflation increase by ≥1.1% in April?
markets.description:
This is a market about the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

This market will resolve to the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) in April 2026 according to the monthly BLS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for April 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on May 12, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) which BLS reports to one decimal point (e.g. 0.4%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

markets.question: Game Handicap: TEX (-2.5) vs Toronto KOI (+2.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Call of Duty match between Toronto KOI and OpTic Texas in the Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers, initially scheduled for May 2 at 7:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "OpTic Texas" if OpTic Texas wins 3 or more games than Toronto KOI in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Toronto KOI". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Game Handicap: TEX (-2.5) vs Toronto KOI (+2.5)

events.title: Call of Duty: Toronto KOI vs OpTic Texas (BO5) - Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers
events.description:
This market refers to the Call of Duty match between Toronto KOI and OpTic Texas in the Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers, initially scheduled for May 2 at 7:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Toronto KOI" if Toronto KOI win the match against OpTic Texas.

This market will resolve to "OpTic Texas" if OpTic Texas win the match against Toronto KOI.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: OpTic Texas holds a dominant edge over Toronto KOI in recent head-to-heads, sweeping their last six series 0-6 with a 18-2 map advantage, capped by a 3-1 elimination win in the CDL Minor I tournament on April 25 where Mercules and Shotzzy delivered hardpoint masterclasses on maps like Scar and Den. Both teams entered Stage 3 Major Qualifiers strongly—Toronto KOI at 2-0 (6-2 map record) after wins over Riyadh Falcons and others, OpTic Texas 1-0 (3-1)—fueled by stable rosters featuring Shotzzy, Dashy, Huke, and Mercules for Texas, and Insight, CleanX, JoeDeceives, Kips for KOI. Recent scrims showed competitiveness (e.g., 4-3, 5-5 map splits), but trader consensus reflects Texas' superior form, meta adaptation on Black Ops 6 maps like Gridlock and Exposure, and momentum heading into this BO5 qualifier bracket match.

markets.question: Will Nolan McLean win the 2026 NL Cy Young Award?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 National League Cy Young Award.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLB rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Baseball; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Skenes' dominant strikeout rates and Pirates rotation anchor role mirror his breakout form, while King's high-volume innings and K/9 for the Padres, Ohtani's post-TJ velocity resurgence with the Dodgers, and Misiorowski's prospect hype via Brewers debut flashes have fueled the bunching. markets.question: Will US crude oil reserves fall to 275M by June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

If data has not been released for the final week ending on or before June 5 2026, by June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: US commercial crude oil inventories fell sharply by 6.2 million barrels to 459.5 million barrels in the week ending April 24 per the latest EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report, now just 1% above the five-year average after early April builds pushed stocks toward three-year highs. This drawdown, alongside refinery crude runs rising to 16.1 million barrels per day at 89.6% utilization, reflects stronger processing ahead of summer driving season demand. US production holds near record 13.5 million barrels per day, with exports and imports key variables. Traders eye weekly EIA reports through June 3 for sustained drawdowns needed to reach lower thresholds by June 5 resolution, amid OPEC+ modest May output hikes and global supply tensions.

markets.question: Spread: AS Monaco FC (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 2 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "AS Monaco FC" if AS Monaco FC win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Metz".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligue1.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Metz vs. AS Monaco FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 2 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: FC Metz sit 18th in Ligue 1 with 26 points from 30 matches, mired in a relegation scrap, hosting seventh-placed AS Monaco on May 2 at Stade Saint-Symphorien amid a mounting injury crisis including Maxime Colin (calf), Boubacar Traoré (knee), and Believe Munongo out. Metz's porous defense concedes heavily, worsened by recent struggles against bottom-table rivals. Monaco, with 49 points from 29 games, endured a 4-1 loss to Paris FC last week despite Folarin Balogun extending his scoring streak to nine straight appearances and Paul Pogba's injury return, but remain potent away with an unbeaten run in eight head-to-heads versus Metz, capped by September's 5-2 win. Monaco's long-term absentees like Takumi Minamino (knee) and Mohammed Salisu (cruciate) test depth, yet their form and matchup edge shape trader consensus toward visiting superiority in this table-climbing fixture.

Greece's Akylas ("Ferto"), Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund ("Før vi går hjem"), France's Monroe ("Regarde!"), Israel’s Noam Bettan ("Michelle"), and Australia’s Delta Goodrem ("Eclipse") form a tight pack in the 47-53% implied probability range, balancing strong jury appeal with televote potential amid competitive semi-final draws. All 35 entries are confirmed despite boycotts from five nations; staging reveals and semi-final qualifiers on May 12-14 will be pivotal momentum shifters before the May 16 grand final.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 86% due to the absence of federal momentum for stringent AI safety legislation in the divided 119th Congress, where no bill matching resolution criteria—such as prohibitions on AI model creation, training restrictions, usage bans, or mandatory human-in-the-loop requirements—has advanced beyond introduction. Recent bipartisan efforts, including Reps. Lieu and Obernolte's April 27 American Leadership in AI Act consolidating prior proposals and Senate bills targeting child AI chatbot restrictions and deepfake distribution, focus on innovation promotion and narrow protections rather than comprehensive safety mandates. The Trump administration's March National AI Policy Framework emphasizes light-touch regulation and state preemption, while states enact their own measures amid federal inaction, leaving slim odds for enactment by December 31, 2026.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will US withdraw from NATO by...?

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's April 2026 interviews and administration signals have reignited debate over potential US withdrawal from NATO, primarily due to European allies' refusal to join US-Israel military actions against Iran, including efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. However, a 2024 National Defense Authorization Act provision bars unilateral presidential exit, requiring two-thirds Senate approval or joint congressional resolution, presenting formidable legal barriers. NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte dismissed withdrawal fears on April 19, emphasizing alliance resilience. Trader consensus reflects these institutional hurdles alongside historical precedents of unfulfilled threats, with no scheduled congressional votes or summits imminent to advance any exit process before year-end resolution windows.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will Baidu have the second best AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the second-highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will Trump post "Prosecute" or "Prosecution" on Truth Social this week?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if @realDonaldTrump posts/truths the listed term between April 27, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, all text posted by the listed account in quote and reply posts/truths count toward a "Yes" resolution, but quoted posts/truths and reposts/reTruths will not count.

Text posted in images, memes, or other non-animated, non-video media that are not strictly text will qualify towards a "Yes" resolution only if the listed term is spelled out clearly and in full. (e.g., words spelled out in a letter posted as a .jpg will qualify, however a word posted as part of an animated .gif will not.)

Any plural or possessive forms of a listed term, as well as variance in capitalizations, will count toward the resolution of this market, regardless of context. Other forms of the listed term will NOT count.

Extraneous symbols being inserted into a word (ex: r@d1cal, for "radical") will disqualify it from counting toward a "Yes" resolution. Sigils at the beginnings of words, such as hashtags, "@" symbols, or denotations of currency, will not disqualify a term from counting towards a "Yes" resolution.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

The resolution source for this market will be Donald Trumps's verified Truth Social account: @realDonaldTrump

Please note, only the @realDonaldTrump verified Truth Social account counts for this market, regardless of the URL for this profile. If Donald Trump posts/truths from another account, it has no bearing on the resolution of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's recent Truth Social posts on April 28, claiming Iran is in a "State of Collapse" and demanding the Strait of Hormuz reopening amid the ongoing US blockade, have elevated trader consensus on "Blockade" to 62% and related foreign policy terms like "Hell" and "Make Iran Great Again," reflecting persistent escalation in US-Iran tensions including nuclear warnings aimed at Germany's Chancellor Merz. The ongoing state visit by King Charles III and Queen Camilla, culminating in a White House state dinner on April 28, bolsters 48-50% odds for phrases like "I love the King" and "Prince Harry." High 84% probability on "Poll/Polling" stems from Trump's pattern of amplifying favorable surveys, with midterms at 60% amid 2026 election cycle discussions; resolution awaits May 3 after potential Doral golf event May 2-3.

markets.question: Will Claude go down 3-5 times in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of days in the month of May during which Claude (claude.ai) experiences any downtime.

Claude will be considered to have experienced downtime on a given day if the Claude Status historical uptime page for the claude.ai component (https://status.claude.com/uptime/) displays that day’s status box as any color other than green once the day’s status box is finalized. Daily status boxes for Claude components other than claude.ai will not impact this market’s resolution.

A day will be considered finalized once the following day’s status box has a display color other than the grey color used for days which have not yet begun.

This market will resolve once the final day in May 2026 is finalized. If the final day in May 2026 has not been finalized by June 7, 2026 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the days in May which have been finalized up to that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Claude Status historical uptime page for the claude.ai component (https://status.claude.com/uptime/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors 12+ downtime days for Claude.ai in May at 75.5% implied probability, driven by a streak of frequent outages in late April 2026, including major disruptions on April 28–29 with elevated API error rates and login failures reported on Anthropic's status page. Partial outages spanning multiple hours nearly every day last week—echoing March and early April incidents tied to caching bugs and compute constraints—have eroded confidence in service reliability amid Anthropic's aggressive scaling of large language models like Claude 4.6. While no fixes have been announced, traders eye ongoing monitoring of status.claude.com for at least 5-minute downtimes defining resolution; a sudden stabilization could shift odds lower.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Fed rate hike in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an 83.5% implied probability against a Federal Reserve rate hike in 2026, reflecting the FOMC's steadfast policy stance amid transient inflation pressures. The Fed held the federal funds rate at 3.50%-3.75% in its March 17-18 and April 29 meetings, despite March 2026 CPI surging to 3.3% year-over-year—driven by a 10.9% energy spike from Middle East war-related oil shocks—marking the highest dissent since 1992 among hawkish members. Yet the March dot plot median projects 3.4% by year-end, signaling one potential cut rather than hikes, with labor markets stable and core inflation moderating. Upcoming May FOMC and April CPI release could test this positioning if geopolitical tensions ease.

markets.question: Will G2 Esports qualify to MSI 2026?
markets.description:
The MSI 2026 (Mid-Season Invitational) is scheduled to take place from June 26 to July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.

markets.question: Bitcoin Up or Down - April 30, 2:50PM-2:55PM ET
markets.description:

The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries' incumbency in the deeply Democratic NY-08 district anchors trader consensus at 92% for a Democratic general election win on November 3, 2026, reflecting the seat's strong partisan lean—rated Solid D by Cook Political Report—and Jeffries' consistent landslide margins in prior cycles. No prominent Republican candidates have filed ahead of the June 23 primaries, underscoring the GOP's structural disadvantages in this Brooklyn-Queens stronghold. Recent Democratic primary challengers, including Councilmember Chi Ossé and Tracy Bostic, have filed but show minimal polling traction against the powerful House leader. Upsets remain possible via a surprise GOP heavyweight entrant, a major Jeffries scandal, legal challenges, or midterm turnout surges favoring Republicans.

markets.question: Will Gençlerbirliği SK win on 2026-05-03?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 3, 2026
If Gençlerbirliği SK wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Fatih Karagümrük SK vs. Gençlerbirliği SK
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Süper Lig game, scheduled for Sunday, May 3, 2026 between Fatih Karagümrük SK and Gençlerbirliği SK.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the Trendyol Süper Lig relegation scrap, trader consensus prices Gençlerbirliği SK slightly ahead at 36% implied probability for the May 3 clash at Atatürk Olimpiyat Stadium, with hosts Fatih Karagümrük at 34.5% and draw at 29%, underscoring a razor-thin matchup amid mutual struggles. Karagümrük, rooted in 18th with 21 points after a dismal 1W-1D-3L run in their last five—including a 0-3 home loss to Konyaspor—desperately need points but face injury absences like Daniel Johnson and Moussa Kone. Gençlerbirliği, 15th on 28 points, hold firmer recent form (2W-3L, capped by a 1-0 win over Kocaelispor) yet contend with long-term outs including Moussa Kyabou and Peter Etebo, plus poor away results; even head-to-head (2-2-1 last five) keeps dynamics tightly balanced.

eventMetadata.context_description: David Brock Smith holds a slim lead in trader consensus for the Oregon Republican U.S. Senate primary on May 19, driven by recent endorsements from the Taxpayers Association of Oregon, Willamette Week, and leading GOP gubernatorial candidates, alongside superior fundraising with over $23,000 cash on hand versus Jo Rae Perkins' $1,000 as of late March. Perkins, the 2020 and 2022 nominee with a loyal populist base, maintains strong support amid criticisms of Smith as establishment. The race remains tight in this nine-candidate field due to divided Republican voters between mainstream appeal and insurgent fervor, with further endorsements, voter turnout in mail-in balloting, or late debates potentially creating separation before ballots drop.

markets.question: Will Liverpool finish in the top 4 of the EPL 2025–26 standings?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs finish in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings for the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club officially finishes in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings at the end of the season, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If two or more clubs are tied on points for a top 4 position, resolution will follow the official English Premier League tiebreaking procedures.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed before October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
