markets.question: Will Microsoft (MSFT) close above $435 end of April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on the final trading day of April 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/MSFT/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

Le Mans boast momentum from recent triumphs including a 4-0 rout of Pau FC on April 6 and a 4-3 comeback at Amiens, bolstering their clean-sheet streak. Reims remain competitive but stumbled to a 2-2 draw at Laval last week, compounded by off-field disruption from armed fan threats at their training ground five days ago potentially impacting morale. Reims dominate head-to-head (unbeaten in six, including February's 3-0 Coupe de France win), though Le Mans contend without long-term absentee Jean Vercruysse (cruciate ligament tear).

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 21°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's latest 9-day forecast, updated early April 30, projects a maximum temperature of 25°C for the day, driving trader consensus to 72% implied probability for 25°C or higher amid mainly cloudy conditions with sunny intervals and north-northeasterly winds force 4-5 from an incoming northeast monsoon. This follows April 29's observed peak of 28.4°C at the Observatory, with the seasonal shift to normal-to-above-normal April temperatures tempered by cooler air masses and 55-80% humidity. The 23% odds on exactly 24°C reflect uncertainty in cloud cover limiting peaks, while lower outcomes trail due to warm soil temperatures (26°C) and sea surface at 25°C supporting at least moderate warmth. Next forecast update at 11:30 HKT could refine model consensus.

markets.question: Will Valtteri Bottas be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' W17 has dominated the early 2026 F1 season, securing a 1-2 in Australia where George Russell won ahead of teammate Kimi Antonelli, followed by Antonelli's back-to-back victories in China and Japan to lead the Drivers' Championship 72-63. This form has propelled the Mercedes duo to 41.5% and 31.1% implied probabilities, reflecting trader consensus on their superior race pace, qualifying edge, and Constructors' lead at 135 points versus Ferrari's 90. Russell's experience over the 19-year-old rookie Antonelli tips the scales despite the points gap, while Charles Leclerc (6.6%) trails amid Ferrari's second-place constructors standing. Red Bull's struggles keep Max Verstappen at just 3.3%, with 21 races remaining and Miami GP looming.

markets.question: Will Ted Cruz post 200+ posts from April 28 to May 5, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Ted Cruz (@tedcruz), posts on X between April 28, 12:00 PM ET and May 5, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Ilhan Omar. This consistency, with no announced schedule disruptions, clusters odds tightly around 60-119 posts over the 8-day window, mirroring prior week's implied volume. Separation could emerge from intensified Senate floor votes or Commerce Committee markups boosting output above 120, lighter news cycles pulling below 60, or viral events like ongoing border security debates prompting spikes.

Both hit 156 lbs at weigh-ins absent injury concerns, with Steele's redemption bid and stylistic edges shaping trader consensus amid Fan's momentum.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Trump's 56%-42% 2024 win underscores GOP strength, per Cook Political's Solid R rating, while fragmented Democratic primary field (Zyon Khalifa, Roger Pruitt, David Robinson II, Daniel Shrief) and low funds limit their 17.5% implied probability. No major developments since March 30 filing deadline; June 9 primaries could clarify nominees, with potential runoffs June 23 potentially influencing A, B, and Other outcomes hovering near 50%.

markets.question: Will Club Atlético de Madrid vs. Arsenal FC end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 29, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Wednesday, April 29, 2026 between Club Atlético de Madrid and Arsenal FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: The UEFA Champions League semi-final first leg at Riyadh Air Metropolitano ended 1-1, cementing trader consensus at 100% on Draw as the market resolves to the confirmed result. Arsenal struck first through Viktor Gyökeres' 44th-minute penalty, exploiting a defensive lapse, but Diego Simeone's Atlético Madrid responded swiftly with Julián Álvarez's clinical spot-kick just after halftime, showcasing their trademark resilience and set-piece prowess at home. Both sides prioritized defensive solidity amid high stakes—Atlético's low-block frustrating Arsenal's attack despite their earlier 4-0 league-phase win—leaving the aggregate tied for the Emirates return leg. Late drama remains unlikely barring rare VAR reversals or administrative issues.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve's federal funds target range stands at 3.50%-3.75% following the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, where policymakers held rates steady amid March CPI inflation accelerating to 3.3% year-over-year—above the 2% target—and unemployment easing to 4.3%, signaling labor market resilience. Trader consensus, reflected in CME FedWatch probabilities, prices a mostly flat rate path near 3.6% effective yield through early 2027, aligning with the March dot plot's median projection of low-3% by year-end 2027, though persistent inflation risks could delay cuts. Key catalysts ahead include June 16-17 FOMC deliberations and May 12 CPI release, with Treasury yields stable around 4% influencing rate expectations.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Moore and signing DE Bradley Chubb, despite a middling D+ draft grade with edge rusher T.J. Parker and CB Davison Igbinosun. Brown landing there, narrowing the gap in this closely contested market. markets.question: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Anyone's Legend and Weibo Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 11 or more kills than Weibo Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Weibo Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

Weibo Gaming secured a morale-boosting 2-0 over Top Esports but sits precariously at 3-6 amid roster upheaval, losing jungler Tian and support Erha in late March. AL boasts a 57% head-to-head win rate, including a 2-0 earlier this split, with Tarzan's jungle pathing and macro sidelane pressure key factors. Top-four spots offer direct playoff berths, heightening stakes under Patch 26.7 meta shifts.

markets.question: Les Wexner charged by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Leslie Wexner between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects an 84% implied probability that Les Wexner will not face criminal charges by June 30, driven by the absence of any DOJ or FBI indictment announcements despite heightened scrutiny. Recent civil lawsuits filed in early April by Jeffrey Epstein victims against Wexner and his foundation allege enabling Epstein's activities but have prompted no known criminal referrals. Earlier February House Oversight Committee deposition and unredacted FBI documents labeling Wexner a 2019 co-conspirator yielded denials of knowledge and no subsequent prosecution, underscoring procedural hurdles like evidentiary thresholds and potential statutes of limitations in sex-trafficking cases. With no scheduled hearings or special counsel probes signaled, traders see low risk of charges materializing soon.

markets.question: Will Africa win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Which continent will win the 2026 Fifa World Cup?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Europe at 73% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, driven by its unmatched depth of 16 qualifiers—including powerhouses Spain, France, England, Germany, Portugal, and Netherlands—that dominated UEFA playoffs through late March, securing spots for Bosnia and Herzegovina, Sweden, Türkiye, and Czechia amid dramatic penalty shootouts. South America's 21% reflects the strength of six CONMEBOL nations like defending champions Argentina, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Paraguay, and Uruguay, though fewer entries limit their knockout path compared to UEFA's volume. Recent group draw placements offer Europe favorable matchups, such as Germany's Group E with Ecuador, Côte d'Ivoire, and Curaçao, while mounting injury concerns like Lamine Yamal's hamstring (expected recovery) and Rodrygo's issue test squad rotations across continents, but Europe's bench depth sustains dominance. Africa (10 teams), Asia (9), North America (6), and Oceania (1) trail as competitive group-stage threats lacking later-round firepower.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: SINQU vs The Last Resort (BO1) - ESEA Advanced Europe Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between SINQU and The Last Resort in the ESEA Advanced Europe Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 29 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "SINQU" if SINQU win the match against The Last Resort.

This market will resolve to "The Last Resort" if The Last Resort win the match against SINQU.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump's tie be another color on April 29, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on the predominant color of the tie worn by Donald Trump during his first public appearance during which he wears a tie on the specified date (ET).

The color will be determined based solely on the first publicly available photo or video of the appearance. Reporting of the tie color without photos or videos will not be considered. Subsequent publication of other photos or videos, including those taken earlier, will not affect the resolution.

For the tie to be considered predominantly blue or red, its primary color must be a shade of blue or red, though it may feature other colors as part of a pattern. 

This tie would count as "Blue": 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/donald-trump-tie-color-blue-5ywRT5OXfn-5.jpg

This tie would count as "Red": 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/donald-trump-tie-color-red-a7ObxtpGQcrR.jpg

The following tie has no distinguishable predominant color, and thus would resolve to "Other": 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/donald-trump-tie-color-other-Q8aIgOP7wFs1.jpg

If Donald Trump does not have any public appearance on the specified date, does not wear a tie, or if it is a color not listed among the strike options, the market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight competition between Red (30%) and Other (30%), with Blue trailing at 20%, driven by President Trump's signature power-red ties clashing against his recent diversification into unconventional shades like metallic purple—seen at February's Board of Peace meeting and yesterday's White House beehive tour with Melania's matching purple hat. markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Buenos Aires be 22°C on April 29?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Minister Pistarini Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 29 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Minister Pistarini Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ar/ezeiza/SAEZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

Morning observations at key stations like Aeroparque show lows near 12–13°C under partly cloudy skies and moderate westerly winds of 20–30 km/h, conditions conducive to daytime warming to precisely 23°C per SMN's hourly projections and model consensus. This aligns with late-April climatology, where average highs hover around 22°C amid a transitioning high-pressure system. Realistic challenges include unexpected persistent cloud cover suppressing peaks to 22°C or brief sunbursts pushing toward 24°C, with final resolution hinging on evening observations from official SMN records.

markets.question: Russell 2000 (RUT) Up or Down on April 29?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the official Russell 2000 Index closing price for Russell 2000 (RUT) on Wednesday, April 29, 2026 is higher than the official Russell 2000 Index closing price for RUT on the most recent prior trading day.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the official Russell 2000 Index closing price for Russell 2000 (RUT) on Wednesday, April 29, 2026 is lower than the official Russell 2000 Index closing price for RUT on the most recent prior trading day.

E.g., ordinarily, a market on Monday would refer to the previous Friday for its most recent closing price, unless that Friday were a market holiday, in which case it would refer to Thursday, or the next most recent trading day.

If the two specified closing prices are exactly equal, this market will resolve 50-50. Note that all figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.

If RUT does not trade at all during the regular session, the market will resolve 50-50.

If either of the relevant days are shortened (for example, due to a market holiday schedule), the official closing price published by Russell 2000 Index for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If either of the relevant days have no official closing price (for example, due to a trading halt into the market close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is the Wall Street Journal, specifically the Close values published by the WSJ under "Historical Prices".

US: https://www.wsj.com/market-data/stocks
EMEA: https://www.wsj.com/market-data/stocks/emea 
ASIA: https://www.wsj.com/market-data/stocks/asia

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

markets.question: Will DeepSeek have the #3 AI model at the end of April 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the third-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies third place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will the median home value in Miami be less than $1,180,000 on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the median home value for all property types in Miami, Florida on April 30, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket. 

The resolution source will be official data from the Parcl Labs Sales Price Index for Miami City. The settlement price will be calculated by multiplying the published price index value (price per square foot) by 2100, which is the median square footage in Miami. Parcl is set to publish this data on April 30, 2026. If no data for April 30 is released by May 10, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve according to the most recently published data. (see: https://app.parcllabs.com/prediction-market-resolutions/23)

markets.question: Elon Bull Run Parlay
markets.description:

- Elon Musk trillionaire
- Another Elon baby
- 9+ SpaceX Starship launches reach space

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will remain open until it is confirmed that at least one of the above conditions has not been met, or until all of the above conditions have been met within the specified timeframe.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: Aryna Sabalenka leads trader consensus at 34.5% implied probability for the 2026 US Open title, driven by her world No. 1 ranking, unbeaten streak through 25-1 in 2026, and hard court dominance with WTA 1000 wins at Indian Wells and Miami earlier this year. Elena Rybakina at 18.7% follows closely as No. 2 with Australian Open triumph and a fresh Stuttgart clay title over Karolina Muchova, bolstering her all-surface threat on the Flushing Meadows hard courts. Iga Swiatek trails at 16.0% amid hard court vulnerabilities, including a shock Miami second-round loss to Magda Linette and Stuttgart quarterfinal exit to Mirra Andreeva. Xiyu Wang's 11.8% reflects her 13-1 start and career-high No. 49 ranking from recent breakthroughs, while Coco Gauff lags despite No. 3 status due to inconsistent spring results. Sabalenka's Madrid clay progress post-Stuttgart injury withdrawal underscores her resilience heading into summer hard courts.

markets.question: Will the Bank of Russia decrease the key rate after the July Meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the key rate resulting from the Bank of Russia’s July meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Russia after its July 24, 2026 meeting as listed on the official Bank of Russia calendar: https://www.cbr.ru/eng/dkp/cal_mp/#t13

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Russia’s press release for their July 24, 2026 meeting with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the key rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders' consensus prices a razor-thin edge for no change (52%) over a key rate decrease (48.5%) at the Bank of Russia's July 2026 meeting, reflecting deep uncertainty after the central bank's cautious 50 basis point cut to 14.5% on April 24 amid March inflation holding steady at 5.9% year-over-year, above the 4% target. Persistent price pressures from a tight labor market and fiscal spending clash with early-year GDP contraction of 1.8% in January-February, prompting debate on whether accumulated monetary policy tightness suffices to anchor inflation toward 4% in the second half of 2026 or if further easing is needed. The June meeting and intervening CPI releases will be pivotal swing factors.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) hit (LOW) $264 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Apple (AAPL) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

Analyst consensus points to a $298 average 12-month price target (high $350), reflecting optimism on FY2026 growth to $466 billion revenue, though UBS holds neutral at $287 citing memory cost pressures. Post-earnings guidance will shape May trajectories, with China sales and regulatory scrutiny as key risks; WWDC in June looms as the next catalyst for valuation shifts.

Ralph Alvarado commands 80.5% trader consensus as the Republican nominee for Kentucky's open 6th Congressional District, fueled by his fundraising dominance reported last week alongside Greg Plucinski and key endorsements like the U.S. Chamber of Commerce. In the closed primary on May 19, Alvarado's name recognition from prior legislative service and America First positioning solidify his frontrunner status amid a crowded field lacking public polling. markets.question: Will both candidates advancing to the general election for Governor of California from the June 2, 2026 primary be affiliated with the Republican Party?
markets.description:
The non-partisan primary election for Governor of California is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026. The top two candidates in this election by number of votes won will advance to the general election for Governor of California.

This market will resolve according to the party affiliation of the listed candidates who advance from the primary to the general election for Governor of California. A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with a party.

In case any combination of parties advances that is not listed here, including all scenarios where an independent candidate advances, this market will resolve to "Other".

If no 2026 California gubernatorial primary takes place by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the results of the primary election for Governor of California as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the government of California, specifically the Office of the Secretary of State.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors a Democrat-Republican top-two matchup at 73% implied probability for California's June 2 nonpartisan primary, driven by recent polls showing Republicans Steve Hilton and Chad Bianco consolidating GOP support amid a fragmented Democratic field of eight major contenders. Eric Swalwell's mid-April dropout boosted Xavier Becerra to 21% in the latest EMC Research survey (April 21-26), narrowly ahead of Hilton at 20%, Steyer at 17%, and Bianco at 15%, with 26% undecided and economy as the top issue at 41%. The April 22 debate highlighted divisions without consolidating Democrats, while prior Emerson and EVITARUS polls had Hilton and Bianco leading outright, underscoring risks of Dem-Dem (15%) if GOP turnout lags or Rep-Rep (5.5%) if Republicans dominate. Ballots mail May 4.

events.title: CS Cienciano vs. CD Comerciantes Unidos
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Peru Liga 1 game, scheduled for Saturday, May 2, 2026 between CS Cienciano and CD Comerciantes Unidos.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cienciano's commanding home form at high-altitude Estadio Inca Garcilaso de la Vega in Cusco, where they've secured five wins and one draw without a loss this Liga 1 Apertura season, anchors trader consensus favoring them at 57.5% implied probability. Sitting third in the table ahead of fifth-placed Comerciantes Unidos, Cienciano boasts a dominant head-to-head record with four wins in six meetings and recent momentum from five straight league victories before a 2-2 draw at UTC last weekend. Comerciantes Unidos remain competitive after a 1-0 upset over Sporting Cristal but face away challenges against the altitude and hosts' defense, boosting the draw to 26.5% while limiting their win chance to 18%. Right winger Juan Romagnoli's injury tempers Cienciano slightly, yet no major absences for visitors.

markets.question: Map Handicap: LYON (-2.5) vs Contra (+2.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Valorant Grand final match between LYON and Contra in the VCL Latin America North: ACE Masters Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 29 at 6:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "LYON" if LYON wins 3 or more maps than Contra in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Contra". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Valorant: LYON vs Contra (BO5) - VCL Latin America North: ACE Masters Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Valorant Grand final match between LYON and Contra in the VCL Latin America North: ACE Masters Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 29 at 6:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "LYON" if LYON win the match against Contra.

This market will resolve to "Contra" if Contra win the match against LYON.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: LYON heads into the VCL Latin America North ACE Masters playoffs grand final on a dominant run, topping the regular season standings undefeated at 7-0 before sweeping Fuego 2-0 in semis with a comeback on Haven and Dragon earning MVP honors via clutch 4Ks. Contra punched their ticket with a hard-fought 2-1 semifinal victory over second-ranked Tezcat Gaming, building resilience after an earlier 3-0 playoff loss to LYON this stage. Both squads boast stable rosters—LYON (Feniz, Kess, Jow, Mephisto, Dragon) post stand-in recovery, Contra (Peloncito, CRUNCH, dedmos, gioØ2x, paganus)—setting up a pivotal BO5 where LYON's map control and economy edge contrast Contra's upset potential in the current meta.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in London be 17°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Met Office's latest forecast update, issued late on April 29, projects a maximum temperature of 21°C in London on April 30 under persistent high pressure delivering sunny skies and minimal precipitation risk, driving trader consensus toward 19°C (47%) and 18°C (31.5%) as leading outcomes. Abundant sunshine—expected for 8-10 hours—combined with light-to-moderate winds (gusts to 30mph) supports rapid daytime warming from overnight lows near 9-10°C, though gusts may temper the "feels like" to 15°C. This aligns with BBC guidance at 20°C and extends early April's record warmth (26°C peaks), well above the long-term April average of 13-15°C. Model consensus holds firm, but minor cloud incursions or wind shifts could adjust outcomes; hourly observations from Heathrow and city stations will resolve the market by midnight.

events.title: Al Okhdood SC vs. Al Ettifaq Saudi Club - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Saudi Professional League game, scheduled for April 30 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Al Okhdood, languishing in 17th place with just 16 points from 28 Saudi Pro League matches and a league-worst -36 goal difference, host mid-table 7th-placed Al Ettifaq (42 points from 29 games) on April 30 at Prince Hathloul Stadium in a relegation six-pointer. Okhdood's dismal form continued with a 0-2 home defeat to leaders Al Nassr on April 11, exposing defensive frailties, while Ettifaq stumbled 1-0 at Al Nassr last week after a thrilling 3-2 comeback win over Al Qadsiah. Ettifaq holds head-to-head edge, winning three of five prior meetings, though Okhdood's home desperation and no major reported injuries for either side could fuel an upset; trader consensus reflects Ettifaq's superior squad depth and away form amid late-season table pressures.
